2603.13452.title: MESD: Detecting and Mitigating Procedural Bias in Intersectional Groups
2603.13452.paper_summary: Research about bias in machine learning has mostly focused on outcome-oriented fairness metrics (e.g., equalized odds) and on a single protected category. Although these approaches offer great insight into bias in ML, they provide limited insight into model procedure bias. To address this gap, we proposed multi-category explanation stability disparity (MESD), an intersectional, procedurally oriented metric that measures the disparity in the quality of explanations across intersectional subgroups in multiple protected categories. MESD serves as a complementary metric to outcome-oriented metrics, providing detailed insight into the procedure of a model. To further extend the scope of the holistic selection model, we also propose a multi-objective optimization framework, UEF (Utility-Explanation-Fairness), that jointly optimizes three objectives. Experimental results across multiple datasets show that UEF effectively balances objectives. Also, the results show that MESD can effectively capture the explanation difference between intersectional groups. This research addresses an important gap by examining explainability with respect to fairness across multiple protected categories.
2603.13452.paper_summary_zh: 研究機器學習中的偏見主要集中在以結果為導向的公平性指標（例如，平等機會）和單一保護類別。雖然這些方法對機器學習中的偏見提供了很好的洞見，但對模型程序偏見的洞察則有限。為了解決這一缺口，我們提出了多類別解釋穩定性差異（MESD），這是一個交叉性、程序導向的指標，衡量多個保護類別中交叉子群體解釋質量的差異。MESD作為一個補充指標，提供了對模型程序的詳細洞察，與以結果為導向的指標相輔相成。為了進一步擴展整體選擇模型的範疇，我們還提出了一個多目標優化框架UEF（效用-解釋-公平性），該框架共同優化三個目標。多個數據集的實驗結果顯示，UEF有效平衡了各項目標。此外，結果顯示MESD能有效捕捉交叉群體之間的解釋差異。本研究通過考察多個保護類別的公平性與可解釋性之間的關係，填補了一個重要的空白。
2603.13452.author: Gideon Popoola et.al.
